heading: Organization of Commission
label: 4274 Organization of Commission
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to prior to the general revision and enactment of Title 5, Government Organization and Employees, by , , .section 2374 of Title 5Pub. L. 89–554, § 180 Stat. 378Sept. 6, 1966
amendments: CodificationSection was formerly classified to prior to the general revision and enactment of Title 5, Government Organization and Employees, by , , .section 2374 of Title 5Pub. L. 89–554, § 180 Stat. 378Sept. 6, 1966

children.heading: Initial meeting
children.label: a Initial meeting
children.text: The President shall convene the Commission within ninety days following at such time and place as he may designate for the Commission’s initial meeting.September 24, 1959

children.heading: Chairman and Vice Chairman
children.label: b Chairman and Vice Chairman
children.text: The President shall designate a Chairman and a Vice Chairman from among members of the Commission.

children.heading: Vacancies in membership
children.label: c Vacancies in membership
children.text: Any vacancy in the membership of the Commission shall be filed in the same manner in which the original appointment was made; except that where the number of vacancies is fewer than the number of members specified in paragraphs (4), (5), (6), and (7) of , each panel of names submitted in accordance with the aforementioned paragraphs shall contain at least two names for each vacancy.section 4273(a) of this title

children.heading: Termination of service in official position from which originally appointed
children.label: d Termination of service in official position from which originally appointed
children.text: Where any member ceases to serve in the official position from which originally appointed under , his place on the Commission shall be deemed to be vacant.section 4273(a) of this title

children.heading: Quorum
children.label: e Quorum
children.text: Thirteen members of the Commission shall constitute a quorum, but two or more members shall constitute a quorum for the purpose of conducting hearings.
